signals.headline: Ripple Unveils Quantum-Resistant Roadmap for XRPL by 2028** | YES | HIGH confidence
signals.raw:
Ripple Unveils Quantum-Resistant Roadmap for XRPL by 2028** | YES | HIGH confidence

Ripple announced a four-phase roadmap on April 20, 2026, to make the XRP Ledger (XRPL) **quantum-ready by 2028**[2][4]. Phase 3, beginning in the second half of 2026, involves testing hybrid signature schemes combining post-quantum and classical cryptography on Devnet[2]. This positions XRPL ahead of Bitcoin and E

signals.headline: XRP ETF Inflows Signal Institutional Adoption** | YES | HIGH confidence
signals.raw:
XRP ETF Inflows Signal Institutional Adoption** | YES | HIGH confidence

XRP outperformed major cryptocurrencies with a **6.7% weekly gain** driven by strong institutional demand[2]. US ETF inflows totaled $38.86 million over four consecutive days, with XRP-linked funds attracting nearly $56 million in a six-day inflow streak and $65.89 million in April alone[2][6]. signals.headline: SEC Regulatory Roundtable on CLARITY Act** | YES | MEDIUM confidence
signals.raw:
SEC Regulatory Roundtable on CLARITY Act** | YES | MEDIUM confidence

The SEC scheduled a landmark roundtable for April 16, 2026, to discuss the CLARITY Act, which aims to clarify whether the SEC or CFTC will oversee digital assets[2]. However, the Senate Banking Committee failed to schedule a markup vote by the April 20-24 window, raising concerns the bill may miss its April deadline[3][7].
